reason: From the available assumptions WP only knows j <= 6 (plus typing/range facts). That is insufficient to prove equality, and in fact the loop exit condition gives j < i, which together with the invariant i+2*j==21 implies j=6 (and i=9). The failure indicates the current loop invariant is too weak (missing the complementary fact relating i and j at loop exit), so the post-loop assertion is currently not derivable.

needed_bridge_facts: at loop exit: j < i (negation of loop condition)
combine with invariant i + 2*j == 21 to conclude j == 6
raw.suggested_fix: Strengthen the loop invariant (or add an additional invariant) so that together with the negated loop condition it entails j==6, e.g., track a relation such as i == 21 - 2*j (equivalent) and add bounds/ordering facts allowing derivation at exit.
